text:
We can model each bulb by its number.  
Bulb \( n \) is toggled on rounds that are divisors of \( n \).  
It ends on if toggled an odd number of times, i.e., if \( n \) has an odd number of divisors.  

A number has an odd number of divisors exactly when it is a perfect square.  

From 1 to 100, the perfect squares are:  
\( 1^2, 2^2, \dots, 10^2 \) → 10 numbers.  

Thus, 10 bulbs are on.

**Final answer:** 10
